[cron:033adb0b-f08b-47e9-a0f6-a71bfe729a9d 每日 skill 升级检查] 执行每日 skill 升级检查。

1. 运行 `clawhub list` 查看当前已装的 skill
2. 运行 `clawhub update --all --no-input --force` 升级所有 skill
3. 如果有 skill 升级失败，记下错误信息
4. 汇报格式：
   - 检查时间（Asia/Shanghai）
   - 升级成功的 skill（列出版本号变化）
   - 升级失败的 skill（列出错误）
   - 已是最新、没动的 skill（仅列数量）
5. 如果所有 skill 都已是最新，回复一句话即可，不要冗长

注意：
- 不要主动修改配置文件
- 任何破坏性操作前先 stop 并汇报

content.thinking: The clawhub update command is failing consistently. It seems like there's a rate limiting or cooldown on the clawhub registry. Let me try reading the clawhub skill to understand how it works.

content.text:
---
name: clawhub
description: "Search, install, update, sync, or publish agent skills with the ClawHub CLI and registry."
metadata:
  {
    "openclaw":
      {
        "requires": { "bins": ["clawhub"] },
        "install":
          [
            {
              "id": "node",
              "kind": "node",
              "package": "clawhub",
              "bins": ["clawhub"],
              "label": "Install ClawHub CLI (npm)",
            },
          ],
      },
  }
---

# ClawHub CLI

Install

```bash
npm i -g clawhub
```

Auth (publish)

```bash
clawhub login
clawhub whoami
```

Search

```bash
clawhub search "postgres backups"
```

Install

```bash
clawhub install my-skill
clawhub install my-skill --version 1.2.3
```

Update (hash-based match + upgrade)

```bash
clawhub update my-skill
clawhub update my-skill --version 1.2.3
clawhub update --all
clawhub update my-skill --force
clawhub update --all --no-input --force
```

List

```bash
clawhub list
```

Publish

```bash
clawhub publish ./my-skill --slug my-skill --name "My Skill" --version 1.2.0 --changelog "Fixes + docs"
```

Notes

- Default registry: https://clawhub.com (override with CLAWHUB_REGISTRY or --registry)
- Default workdir: cwd (falls back to OpenClaw workspace); install dir: ./skills (override with --workdir / --dir / CLAWHUB_WORKDIR)
- Update command hashes local files, resolves matching version, and upgrades to latest unless --version is set

content.text:
---
name: self-improvement
description: "Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks."
metadata:
---

# Self-Improvement Skill

Log learnings and errors to markdown files for continuous improvement. Coding agents can later process these into fixes, and important learnings get promoted to project memory.

## First-Use Initialisation

Before logging anything, ensure the `.learnings/` directory and files exist in the project or workspace root. If any are missing, create them:

```bash
mkdir -p .learnings
[ -f .learnings/LEARNINGS.md ] || printf "# Learnings\n\nCorrections, insights, and knowledge gaps captured during development.\n\n**Categories**: correction | insight | knowledge_gap | best_practice\n\n---\n" > .learnings/LEARNINGS.md
[ -f .learnings/ERRORS.md ] || printf "# Errors\n\nCommand failures and integration errors.\n\n---\n" > .learnings/ERRORS.md
[ -f .learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md ] || printf "# Feature Requests\n\nCapabilities requested by the user.\n\n---\n" > .learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md
```

# Self-Improvement Skill

Log learnings and errors to markdown files for continuous improvement. Coding agents can later process these into fixes, and important learnings get promoted to project memory.

## First-Use Initialisation

Before logging anything, ensure the `.learnings/` directory and files exist in the project or workspace root. If any are missing, create them:

```bash
mkdir -p .learnings
[ -f .learnings/LEARNINGS.md ] || printf "# Learnings\n\nCorrections, insights, and knowledge gaps captured during development.\n\n**Categories**: correction | insight | knowledge_gap | best_practice\n\n---\n" > .learnings/LEARNINGS.md
[ -f .learnings/ERRORS.md ] || printf "# Errors\n\nCommand failures and integration errors.\n\n---\n" > .learnings/ERRORS.md
[ -f .learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md ] || printf "# Feature Requests\n\nCapabilities requested by the user.\n\n---\n" > .learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md
```

Let me just accept that:
1. context-engine - registry rate-limited (Skill not found, reset in ~40s) - cannot upgrade
2. self-improving-agent - ambiguous slug (multiple owners) - cannot upgrade with current approach

Both failures are due to external issues (registry rate limiting and ambiguous slug), not local configuration problems.

I should report this to HALEI. The skill upgrade check encountered:
- 2 skills checked
- 0 upgraded successfully  


- 2 failed: context-engine due to registry rate limiting, self-improving-agent due to an ambiguous slug that needs owner specification

I'm debating whether to wait for the registry rate limit to reset and retry, but given that self-improving-agent's slug is inherently ambiguous across multiple owners, that one won't resolve with a simple retry. I'll just report what I've found.

content.text:
Both skills encountered issues that prevent upgrading tonight:

- **context-engine**: registry rate-limited ("Skill not found", resets ~every 45s)
- **self-improving-agent**: slug 存在多作者歧义（@pskoett / @kingaiwork / @nguyenmanhdung-app 三选一，需确认来源）

本次检查失败均为 registry 侧限制，非本地配置问题。建议后续：
1. 等待 rate limit 清退后重试 context-engine
2. 确认 self-improving-agent 的原始来源作者后，用完整引用更新

**2026-07-01 04:00 (Asia/Shanghai) Skill 升级检查结果**
- ✅ 升级成功：0
- ❌ 升级失败：2（context-engine rate-limited；self-improving-agent slug 歧义）
